summary: Small ftCommon damage matching PR. It removed a helper wrapper around a stick-magnitude check in `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag`, used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of direct `gobj->user_data` access in two damage functions, and relabeled several `.sdata2` constants in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` from generic `lbl_` names to compiler-style local `@` symbols. The decomp.dev report confirmed two new full matches: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`, with GALE01 matched code increasing by 804 bytes. Although the PR title also names `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, the available diff excerpt and bot report do not show a source hunk or reported new match for that function.
what_changed: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` now gets the fighter pointer with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` rather than `gobj->user_data`.

A file-local helper named `isPointInCircle(float x, float y, float radius)` was removed. Its logic was inlined into `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` as a local boolean named `isPointInCircle` using `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.
`ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` still gates the SDI/exit-hitlag handling on the stick magnitude test or `ftCo_800DF608(fp)`, but the expression shape changed to match codegen.
`ftCo_Damage_Coll` now also uses `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of direct `gobj->user_data` access before checking `fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground`.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` renamed `.sdata2` entries at `0x804D84D4`, `0x804D84D8`, `0x804D8500`, and `0x804D8508` from `lbl_804D...` names to `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, and `@523`.
The bot report recorded `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` moving from 99.90% to 100.00% and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` from 99.97% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Inlining the small magnitude helper avoided an extra abstraction that apparently disturbed matching; preserving the explicit bool assignment created the shape needed for `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag`.
Using the project macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of raw `gobj->user_data` improved consistency and affected codegen enough to complete matches in both touched functions.
The stick test was expressed with existing math macros, `VEC2_SQ_LEN` and `SQ`, rather than manually summing squared components through a custom helper.

Updating `symbols.txt` alongside the C change kept local floating-point constants aligned with the compiler/decompilation view, replacing anonymous global-looking labels with local `@` names where appropriate.
decomp_lessons: For fighter callbacks that receive `Fighter_GObj*`, try `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` before raw `gobj->user_data`; the macro can be both more idiomatic and better for matching.

A helper function can prevent a match even when its semantics are simple. If the original code likely used an inline expression, rewrite as local conditionals or temporary booleans to reproduce codegen.
For vector magnitude threshold checks, `VEC2_SQ_LEN(vec) >= SQ(threshold)` is a reusable pattern and may match better than passing squared components into a custom predicate.

Symbol-map constant naming can matter for matching and readability. When constants are local to compiler output, use the `@NNN` style shown by surrounding `.sdata2` entries instead of forcing `lbl_804D...` names.
Bot reports are useful ground truth for what actually matched: here the title mentions three functions, but the automated report only confirms two new matches.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_Damage_*` names are used for common fighter damage callbacks such as `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag`, `ftCo_Damage_Coll`, and the title-referenced `ftCo_Damage_Anim`.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the preferred accessor pattern in these fighter callback contexts.
Compiler/local floating constants in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` may use `@NNN` names, e.g. `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, `@523`, instead of address-derived `lbl_804D...` labels.
Project math macro names used here include `VEC2_SQ_LEN` for squared 2D vector length and `SQ` for squaring scalar thresholds.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw `gobj->user_data` loads with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` to test whether macro expansion matches expected instruction/register allocation.
Inline tiny boolean predicates when the original code likely did not emit a callable helper.
Introduce an explicit local `bool` temporary and assign it in an `if/else` rather than collapsing directly into a single expression when matching branch structure matters.
Use squared-distance comparisons to avoid sqrt-style logic and mirror the existing fixed-point/vector macro idioms.
Relabel `.sdata2` constants to local `@` symbols when a function match reveals that the constants should be scoped/named like compiler-generated locals.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The automated decomp.dev comment reported GALE01 matched code improved to 55.49%, a +0.02% change and +804 bytes.
The automated report listed 2 new matches: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `ftCo_Damage_Anim` to verify whether the title-referenced match was completed through symbol relabeling or omitted from the bot report.
Search for other fighter callbacks still using `gobj->user_data` directly where `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` might improve matching.
Search for custom point-in-circle or stick-magnitude helpers that could be replaced by `VEC2_SQ_LEN(vec) >= SQ(threshold)`.
Audit nearby `.sdata2` `lbl_804D...` constants in `ftCo_Damage.c` for candidates that should be local `@NNN` symbols.
